content.problemStatement:
Finally a monsoon has come. According to the Meteorological Department, there will be rain in the upcoming NNN days in the city. Initially, the water level of the city is zero millimetres. The amount of rain on the iii-th day can be described by an integer AiA_iAi​ as follows:


If Ai>0A_i \gt 0Ai​>0, the water level of the city increases by AiA_iAi​ millimetres on the iii-th day.
If Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0, there is no rain on the iii-th day. The water level of the city decreases by DDD millimetres on such a day. However, if the water level is less than DDD millimetres before the iii-th day, then it becomes zero instead.
There will be a red alert in the city if the water level becomes strictly greater than HHH millimetres on at least one of the NNN days. Determine if there will be a red alert.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, DDD and HHH.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if there will be a red alert or `"NO"` otherwise.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains three space‑separated integers N, D and H. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN describing the rain on each day.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the water level ever becomes strictly greater than H, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case never exceeds 6, second exceeds 100 on day 2, third reaches exactly 3 but not more, fourth exceeds 10 on day 3.
All days have no rain, water level stays 0 which never exceeds 4.
Day 1 level=2, day 2 level=0, day 3 level=5 which is >4, so red alert.

hidden.label: edge case with clamp to zero

solution.approach: We simulate the water level day by day. Keep a variable level initially 0. For each Ai: if Ai > 0, add Ai to level; otherwise (Ai == 0) set level = max(level - D, 0). After updating, if level > H we can stop and answer YES for this test case. If the loop finishes without exceeding H, answer NO. The simulation is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, overall O(T·N).
